formatted_summary:
agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Functional causes dominate recurring abdominal pain in kids; prioritize non-organic assessments over extensive testing until alarming symptoms suggest otherwise. Psychological and behavioral aspects should be considered first when evaluating these patients. To address your query directly:

Recurrent abdominal pain in children primarily results from functional issues more so than organ-related problems, focusing initial evaluations on psychology and behavior instead of immediate diagnostic tests absent severe warning signs. Key takeaway: start by exploring potential psychosocial origins. \boxed{\text{A}} Summary: Repeated stomachaches in young ones mainly stem from function-based reasons rather than actual illnesses, prioritizing mental health checks ahead of medical exams without red flags indicating major concerns. Initial assessment should focus on understanding any stress or behaviors contributing to their discomfort. \boxed{\text{A}}
To condense further while maintaining clarity about what matters clinically,

Key Points: 
Functional disorders frequently cause repeated tummy troubles;
Start evaluation with child’s emotions & habits,
Not invasive scans/tests initially if no big worries signaled.\boxed{\text{A}}. Answer choice: B
